steps.title: Find the contrasting clauses
steps.content: The sentence contrasts two complete ideas: markings usually lower a book's value, but when the former owner is Walt Whitman, those markings can be valuable to scholars.

steps.title: Eliminate bad joins
steps.content: Choice B fuses the two clauses with no punctuation or conjunction. Choice C creates a comma splice. Choice D includes the conjunction “but” but omits the comma needed before it in this compound sentence.

steps.title: Join the contrast correctly
steps.content: The remaining choice uses a comma plus “but,” correctly joining two contrasting independent clauses. The answer is A.
